message:
fix(btree,core): return B-tree pages to freelist on DROP TABLE/INDEX (#69)

execute_drop removed tables/indexes from the in-memory schema and
MemDatabase but never called free_page for their B-tree pages. This
left old root pages (and all child/overflow pages) orphaned on disk,
causing stock SQLite's PRAGMA integrity_check to report
"Page N: never used".

Changes:
- Make BtCursor::free_subtree_pages public so Connection can call it
- Add overflow chain freeing to free_subtree_pages (leaf cells)
- Add Connection::free_btree_pages helper that opens a cursor on a
  root page and recursively frees the entire B-tree
- Call free_btree_pages in execute_drop for both TABLE and INDEX drops
- Skip virtual tables (materialized root pages in MemDatabase only)
- Add regression test reproducing the exact Beads migration pattern
  from issue #69

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
